effort_complexity.rationale: Highest-severity slice: the autonomous loop's own dispatch and shared-file safety invariant, plus RTM's golden-thread trace. Three functions in parallel.go plus rtm.go's two field parsers, all the same fix pattern (read board.json via the oracle).

design_decisions.choice: Read tracks/worktree path via the exported board.ReadBoard/BoardRecord and add an unexported local trackInfosFromBoardTracks converter in internal/run, rather than editing internal/board or exporting a new converter.
design_decisions.rationale: internal/board/board.go is T1-drift-guard's exclusive touchpoint this release; editing it from T5 would be an undeclared track collision. board.ReadBoard/BoardRecord/BoardTrack are already exported and read-only-sufficient. Narrow, reversible (a local converter, not a shared-library surface).

design_decisions.choice: Delete parallel.go's parseDocumentedSharedFiles and delegate to router.ParseDocumentedShared, failing open (docShared=nil, non-fatal) when the touchpoint matrix is absent or unparseable.
design_decisions.rationale: parseDocumentedSharedFiles was a weaker second reimplementation matching only the explicit (DOCUMENTED SHARED) marker — the actual AC-03 defect. router.ParseDocumentedShared already does both marker AND >=2-checkmark inference correctly. Fail-open matches the existing oracle-read precedent in the same function. Narrow, reversible.

design_decisions.choice: Read board.json directly in rtm.go via a small local anonymous struct (filepath.Join(releaseDir, board.json)) rather than reconstructing a repo root to call board.ReadBoard or changing Build's signature.
design_decisions.rationale: rtm.Build(releaseDir) has no repoRoot/release split and internal/implement/ready.go is the sole external caller; changing the signature would ripple into an undeclared touchpoint. board.json is a plain sibling of index.md in every real layout. A cross-reference comment to board.Release's JSON tags is added as drift insurance (pin 3). Narrow, reversible.

design_decisions.choice: Retain parseReleaseBenefit/parseOrgObjective as a legacy fallback when board.json is absent (pre-ADR-0009 releases), rather than deleting them like parallel.go's frontmatter helpers.
design_decisions.rationale: Unlike parallel.go's deleted helpers, these still have a live call path for legacy releases with no board.json. Keeping them preserves traceability for un-migrated releases. Narrow, reversible.

design_decisions.choice: Capture AC-06's reachability artefact via a Go-level run.RunParallel invocation against the real release board.json with a no-op RunSliceFn and a throwaway in-memory DB, not the literal `sworn loop --parallel` CLI.
design_decisions.rationale: Coach decision (review.md, option (a)): the literal CLI would dispatch live work against sibling in-flight tracks (T2/T3) and trip swornagent/sworn#46. The Go-level substitute exercises the exact fixed logic (board.ReadBoard/trackInfosFromBoardTracks/documented-shared detection) against real data without side effects. Narrow, reversible.

test_commands: gofmt -l internal/run/parallel.go internal/run/parallel_test.go internal/run/cold_start_test.go internal/rtm/rtm.go internal/rtm/rtm_test.go internal/router/router_test.go
